2604.23079.title: From Pixels to Explanations: Interpretable Diabetic Retinopathy Grading with CNN-Transformer Ensembles, Visual Explainability and Vision-Language Models
2604.23079.paper_summary: The quality of diabetic retinopathy (DR) screening relies on the ability to correctly grade severity; however, many deep-learning (DL) classifiers cannot be easily interpreted in the clinical context. This study presents a methodology that combines strong discriminative models with multimodal explanations, converting retinal pixels into clinically interpretable outputs. Using the APTOS 2019 benchmark, we evaluated six representative CNN- and transformer-based backbones under a controlled protocol with stratified five-fold cross-validation. We then compared ensembling strategies (hard voting, weighted soft voting, stacking) and investigated a hybrid class-level fusion variant to exploit grade-specific advantages. For interpretability, we produced Grad-CAM++ visual attribution maps and short textual rationales using vision-language models (VLMs) conditioned on the fundus image and classifier outputs under conservative prompting constraints. Modern CNN backbones (ResNet-50 and ConvNeXt-Tiny) provided the strongest single-model baselines, with cross-validated QWK up to 0.919 and 0.914, respectively. Ensembling improved ordinal agreement, and weighted soft voting was the most consistent across folds (QWK 0.934 +/- 0.017). Hybrid class-level fusion was competitive but did not yield a statistically reliable improvement over standard fusion in paired fold comparisons (Holm-adjusted p >= 1.000). For explanation quality, Grad-CAM++ offered plausible but coarse localization, and VLM rationales were generally grade-consistent. Quantitatively, VLM variants showed a trade-off between clinical completeness and template-level semantic similarity (coverage 0.700 vs. BERTScore 0.072), while image-text alignment was comparable (CLIPScore approximately 0.34).
2604.23079.paper_summary_zh: 糖尿病視網膜病變 (DR) 篩檢的質量依賴於正確評估嚴重程度的能力；然而，許多深度學習 (DL) 分類器在臨床背景下並不容易解釋。這項研究提出了一種方法，將強大的區分模型與多模態解釋相結合，將視網膜像素轉換為臨床可解釋的輸出。使用 APTOS 2019 基準，我們在受控協議下對六個代表性的 CNN 和 transformer 基礎架構進行了分層五折交叉驗證評估。然後，我們比較了集成策略（硬投票、加權軟投票、堆疊），並研究了一種混合類別級融合變體，以利用特定級別的優勢。為了可解釋性，我們使用基於視覺-語言模型 (VLMs) 的 Grad-CAM++ 可視化歸因圖和簡短文本理由，這些模型以眼底圖像和分類器輸出為條件，並在保守的提示約束下進行。現代 CNN 基礎架構（ResNet-50 和 ConvNeXt-Tiny）提供了最強的單模型基準，交叉驗證的 QWK 分別高達 0.919 和 0.914。集成改善了序數一致性，而加權軟投票在各折中最為一致（QWK 0.934 +/- 0.017）。混合類別級融合具有競爭力，但在配對折比較中未能在統計上可靠地改善標準融合（Holm 調整 p >= 1.000）。就解釋質量而言，Grad-CAM++ 提供了合理但粗略的定位，而 VLM 理由通常與級別一致。在定量方面，VLM 變體顯示出臨床完整性和模板級語義相似性之間的權衡（覆蓋率 0.700 與 BERTScore 0.072），而圖像-文本對齊則相當（CLIPScore 約 0.34）。
2604.23079.author: Pir Bakhsh Khokhar et.al.
2604.23079.authors: Pir Bakhsh Khokhar, Carmine Gravino, Fabio Palomba, Sule Yildirim Yayilgan, Sarang Shaikh
